indicators.label: US Unemployment
indicators.what_it_means: Below 4.5% means tight labor market. Driver wages rising. Harder to recruit.

indicators.label: Avg Hourly Earnings
indicators.what_it_means: Wages are fleet operators' largest cost after fuel. Trending up means margin pressure.

indicators.label: Diesel PPI
indicators.what_it_means: Producer Price Index for diesel. Leading indicator for pump prices in 2-4 weeks.

indicators.label: US Diesel (National)
indicators.what_it_means: National average diesel. Gulf Coast lowest ($3.45), West Coast highest ($4.12).

indicators.label: Coast-to-Coast Spread
indicators.what_it_means: A $0.67 spread means a 200-gallon fill costs $134 more on the West Coast.
